articles.title: Prakash Padukone opens up about Deepika Padukone's depression battle: 'After one of her friends passed away, she became...'
articles.description: Badminton icon Prakash Padukone has spoken about daughter Deepika Padukone's decision to publicly discuss her battle with depression, revealing that the family fully supported her efforts to raise awareness around mental health. He revealed that Deepika's determination to work in the mental health space grew stronger after the loss of a friend.

articles.title: Quote of the day by Aryna Sabalenka: “Sometimes you just need to let it go, to empty it so you’re ready to start over”
articles.description: World number 1 Aryna Sabalenka might have faced a brutal and a shocking loss in the quarterfinals of the French Open on June 3 against Diana Shnaider but she has constantly proven how tough she is even though she has been pretty emotional about it. In the last few hours, the talented player Aryna Sabalenka has expressed her disappointment and frustration as she has even spoken about quitting tennis. While she was spotted screaming on court as she lost the game, she recently spoke about being vocal about her emotions.

articles.title: Forget the players, World Cup's wildest fan Ivana Knoll just made herself the star of the England vs Croatia game
articles.description: Ivana Knoll, the Croatian World Cup superfan, has gone viral after sharing a strange AI-generated football video featuring an England vs Croatia match concept. The clip, posted on Instagram, shows a heavily stylized digital stadium where Knoll appears as part of a surreal, glitch-filled football sequence. Instead of realistic gameplay, the animation includes distorted movement, unrealistic physics, and fashion-focused visuals that quickly caught public attention.

articles.title: LEGO reveals new Lando Norris and Oscar Piastri McLaren Formula 1 helmet sets before Monaco Grand Prix weekend
articles.description: LEGO officially revealed new Formula 1 helmet sets inspired by McLaren drivers Oscar Piastri and Lando Norris before the Monaco Grand Prix weekend. Both sets celebrate McLaren’s 1000th Formula 1 race with special split-color designs. The collectible helmets include exclusive LEGO minifigures, 793 pieces each, and will officially release worldwide on June 3, 2026, according to Bricks Up.

articles.title: What happened to Jay Silva? Cause of death explored following former UFC fighter’s death at 45
articles.description: UFC and Bellator fighter Jay Silva has died at 45 after a 17-year combat sports career. FAME MMA confirmed the news on May 31. Silva fought stars like Chris Leben, Hector Lombard, and Marius Pudzianowski during his career. He also trained with boxing coach Freddie Roach and once said he wanted fans to remember him as “a beast.”

articles.title: Quote of the day for kids by Wayne Gretzky: "You miss 100% of the shots you don't..."
articles.description: Wayne Gretzky, the legendary hockey player, famously said, "You miss 100% of the shots you don't take." This powerful message encourages everyone, especially children, to embrace opportunities and try new activities. Fear of failure should not hold anyone back. Effort leads to growth and confidence. Taking action is key to discovering potential and achieving goals.

articles.description: Teenage cricket sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, who dominated IPL 2026 with record-breaking performances, will be the subject of an academic study by IIM Indore. The research will explore the impact of early fame and pressure on child prodigies, aiming to understand factors driving exceptional talent and how to navigate expectations.

articles.description: Giants quarterback Jaxson Dart navigated a busy Friday. He addressed media about introducing Donald Trump at a rally. Later, he flew to Miami to support his girlfriend Marissa Ayers at her bikini runway show. Ayers' debut went viral. Dart's political appearance had caused locker room tension. Trump praised Dart on Truth Social. The day showcased Dart's eventful NFL offseason.

articles.title: Fact check: Are A.J. Brown’s viral Patriots childhood photos actually fake? Fans have proof
articles.description: New England Patriots player A.J. Brown's attempt at a heartwarming homecoming post backfired spectacularly. Childhood photos shared on Instagram, featuring a Nike logo on a jersey from before the brand's NFL partnership and suspiciously young-looking him, were quickly exposed as AI-generated. This led to widespread mockery, overshadowing his genuine connection to the team.
